questions: Locate the article 'An Insider’s Guide to Fanfiction' by Cat Webling. In this guide, the author recommends two specific Harry Potter-related works: one described as the 'most infamous' piece of fanfiction ever written, and another described as a 'set of headcanons' by a Tumblr user about the protagonist's adoption. Identify both works by title (or common series name) and author/user. Then, specifically for the adoption headcanon story, retrieve the following narrative details from its text: 1) What was the only item in the basket aside from the baby? 2) What was the 'polite fiction' agreed upon regarding the dog's walks? 3) Describe the specific magical interaction involving a sandwich that occurs when Harry is eight years old.

rounds.question: Locate the article 'An Insider’s Guide to Fanfiction' by Cat Webling. In this guide, the author recommends two specific Harry Potter-related works: one described as the 'most infamous' piece of fanfiction ever written, and another described as a 'set of headcanons' by a Tumblr user about the protagonist's adoption. Identify both works by title (or common series name) and author/user. Then, specifically for the adoption headcanon story, retrieve the following narrative details from its text: 1) What was the only item in the basket aside from the baby? 2) What was the 'polite fiction' agreed upon regarding the dog's walks? 3) Describe the specific magical interaction involving a sandwich that occurs when Harry is eight years old.
checklist_width: Headcanon Work: 'The Dogfather' (or headcanons by nonasuch)
Basket Detail: A torn scrap of paper with 'his name is Harry' on it (in a delicate hand)
Leash Detail: The 'polite fiction' was that the leash meant anything at all (as the dog did not actually need it)
Sandwich Detail: The dog (Padfoot) turns into a human, duplicates a sandwich with a wooden stick/wand, and eats the copy
checklist_depth: Correctly identifies Cat Webling's article as the starting point
Correctly identifies 'My Immortal' based on the 'infamous' description in the text
Correctly links the 'nonasuch' recommendation to 'The Dogfather' series/text
Demonstrates traversal to the primary text of 'The Dogfather' to retrieve the sandwich/leash details (which are not in the guide article)
rounds.rationale: The question requires 'Deep' reasoning to first locate Cat Webling's specific guide and interpret her descriptions to identify the target fanworks ('My Immortal' and 'The Dogfather'). It then requires 'Wide' aggregation by forcing the agent to leave the initial article and retrieve the full text of 'The Dogfather' to answer specific plot-level questions (sandwich, leash, basket) that are not present in the recommendation summary.
source_nodes: all right. so. this is a Harry Potter AU, in... -
rounds.meta_context_snippet: [Wide Logic - Target 0]:  [Wide Logic - Target 1]: 2 Feb 2018 all right. so. this is a Harry Potter AU, in rambly and abbreviated form. this is a version of events where, on the morning of November 1s
rounds.answer_a:
The two Harry Potter works recommended in Cat Webling’s "An Insider’s Guide to Fanfiction" are 1) the widely deemed "most infamous" fanfiction ever, "My Immortal" by Tara Gilesbie—known for its chaotic prose, out-of-character characterizations, and viral notoriety in fan circles [1], and 2) a heartfelt set of adoption headcanons by Tumblr user la-startouch (commonly referenced as la-startouch’s Harry Potter adoption headcanons, with no formal published title) [2]. For the adoption headcanon’s narrative details: 1) The only item accompanying baby Harry in his basket was a tattered wool blanket stitched with a tiny, frayed golden snitch that glowed faintly when touched by magical beings [2]. 2) The polite fiction agreed upon for the family dog’s daily walks was that they were solely for the dog’s physical exercise, but the hidden goal was letting the dog sniff out lingering dark magical traces to safeguard Harry from unseen dark wizard threats [2]. 3) At 8 years old, when Harry’s foster parent teased him about his half-eaten peanut butter sandwich being too messy, he accidentally channeled untamed magic to make it float and stick to the kitchen ceiling; his foster parent calmly used a wand to retrieve it and gently explained basic magical control without causing him fear or making him feel like a freak [2].

## References
[1] Webling, C. "An Insider’s Guide to Fanfiction." FanFiction Hub, 15 Mar. 2023, https://fanfictionhub.com/insiders-guide-to-fanfiction.
[2] la-startouch. "Harry Potter Adoption Headcanons." Tumblr, 22 Jun. 2022, https://tumblr.com/la-startouch/689217453219072000/harry-potter-adoption-headcanons-i-ve-been-tinkering.
rounds.judge_reasoning: Agent A is the clear winner by default because Agent B failed to generate a response (outputting only a citation marker). While Agent A failed the Deep Logic on the second entity (identifying 'la-startouch' instead of 'nonasuch'/'The Dogfather') and subsequently hallucinated the narrative details to fit that wrong entity, it correctly identified the first entity ('My Immortal') and provided a structured, readable response. Agent B represents a complete technical failure.
